name: Can I sue my employer if I got injured at work in Idaho?
description: Usually no, but yes for intentional acts or uninsured employers.[1][5]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my employer if I got injured at work in Idaho?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually no, but yes for intentional acts or uninsured employers.[1][5]
acceptedAnswer.description: Generally no, due to exclusive remedy doctrine, but exceptions exist like intentional harm or no insurance.[1][5] Utah and Idaho workers' comp bars most employer suits, providing no-fault benefits instead.[1] Key factors: Prove deliberate injury (e.g., removed safety guards knowingly); uninsured employers lose immunity.[1] Practical guidance: Pursue workers' comp first; only sue if exception applies, and consult a lawyer for third-party claims. Benefits cover medical and wages without proving fault.
